changed_supply_chain_surfaces.rationale: The policy records stage skill references and stage tool scopes, but it does not edit installed global skills or authorize new external skill installation.
The template names skill lifecycle contract fields for future work-item evidence without changing installed skill contents.

sca_or_equivalent.unavailable_tool_disposition: No dependency manifest, dependency version, package-manager script, lockfile, CI or release workflow, fetched prompt, external tool install, executable generated content, or unknown supply-chain surface change is present in the BANDIT-054 implementation. Stage Capability Scope adds local skill-reference policy metadata only.

dependency_manifest_state.rationale: BANDIT-054 adds local Stage Capability Scope validation, command routing, policy metadata, templates, and focused tests without changing dependency manifests or package-manager scripts.

lockfile_state.manifest_rationale: No dependency manifest change is present.

skills.freshness_rule: lifecycle contract reference required when load-bearing skill contracts exist
skills.revocation_path: update or remove the stage declaration in .bandit/policy/stage-capability-scope.json

agent_skills.freshness_rule: validated by npm run bandit -- stage-capability-scope validate --json
agent_skills.revocation_path: remove or revise the corresponding Stage Capability Scope declaration

rationale: BANDIT-054 touches Stage Capability Scope policy and template surfaces that reference stage skills and tools, so explicit supply-chain gate evidence is recorded before aggregate review and landing. The implementation does not touch dependency manifests, lockfiles, package-manager scripts, CI or release workflows, installed global skills, fetched prompts, external tool install paths, executable generated content, or unknown supply-chain surfaces. No operator-supervised supply-chain approval is required before local-record landing.
